features.name: The balance scale
features.description: Shared household tasks drop as weighted pebbles onto a living balance beam. Heavier jobs, heavier pebbles — the beam does the arithmetic, so the invisible load of running a home finally becomes something you can both see.

features.name: The approval inbox
features.description: Even reads the household mail, and Claude turns real bills and appointments into clean drafts in an approval inbox. Nothing becomes shared work or hits the calendar until one of you approves it.

features.name: Money, settled weekly
features.description: Shared receipts stack up through the week and settle 50/50 with one tap. The coin rolls across, the balance reads zero, and no one keeps a mental ledger anymore.

features.name: The Sunday reset
features.description: A ten-minute weekly ritual: look at the week honestly, say one kind thing each, trade what isn't working — then pour the pans out and start the new week even.

features.name: The shared calendar
features.description: Every bill, renewal and appointment lands on a shared household calendar you both subscribe to in Google — colored by who owns what, so nothing falls through the cracks.

features.name: Built for two
features.description: Sign in with Apple, start a household, share one invite code with your partner. Everything — tasks, money, calendar, the weekly reset — is scoped to exactly the two of you.

beats.caption: Only the two of you, ever.
